Recommendations For Best Practices:
We need to find dataset which is more oriented to health sector infrastructure. This can be hard task, as such datasets are usually  not freely available
More time is needed if trainees are not familiar with basic idea of machine learning
Also, it is expected that trainees are familiar with Python programming language and machine learning, otherwise they will not be able to solve practical assignments by themselves.
Recommendations For Expanding: Promote training module on various summer/winter's  school as its focus is more on practical side of anomaly detection.
Recommendations For Future Initiatives:
Incorporate the latest trends, tools, and threat landscapes to ensure participants are equipped to handle current and emerging cybersecurity threats.
Implement regular testing and certifications to evaluate skill levels and identify areas requiring further development.
Encourage team-based exercises and knowledge-sharing forums to build a community of learners who can exchange ideas and strategies.
Q15_Value:
I ran into a very common workshop challenge — too many participants with varying skill levels and not enough structure for everyone to keep up, especially with something as technical as autoencoders for anomaly detection.
In the future:
- give them a working notebook that already runs end-to-end with minimal code changes needed (e.g., only changing a few parameters).
- add “challenge cells” where more advanced students can modify architecture, play with hyperparameters, or try alternative approaches (e.g., variational autoencoder).
-  have one or two assistants in the room to help those who fall behind

Recommendations For Best Practices:
More Longer Format Training with Interactive Exercises: Incorporate hands-on simulations, case studies, and group activities to reinforce learning.
Diverse Learning Styles: Cater to different learning styles by offering a mix of visual, auditory, and kinesthetic content.
Recommendations For Expanding:
(1) Partnerships and Collaborations
Industry Partnerships: Collaborate with critical infrastructure sectors to co-develop training content and reach their employees directly.
Academic Institutions: Partner with universities and colleges to offer the training as part of cybersecurity programs.

(2) Delivery Method Diversification
Online Learning: Develop online modules for flexible learning and broader accessibility.
Microlearning: Offer short, focused training modules for specific topics.
Blended Learning: Combine online and in-person training for optimal learning outcomes.

(3) Marketing and Promotion
Targeted Marketing: Identify and reach specific target audiences through tailored marketing campaigns.
Content Marketing: Create valuable content (e.g., blog posts, webinars) to attract potential participants.
Social Media: Leverage social media platforms to promote the training and engage with the target audience.
Recommendations For Future Initiatives:
Besides above recommendations:
More Longer Format Training with Interactive Exercises: To reinforce learning, incorporate hands-on simulations, case studies, and group activities.
Diverse Learning Methods including face-to-face, online or blended model.

Recommendations For Best Practices:
I ran into a very common workshop challenge — too many participants with varying skill levels and not enough structure for everyone to keep up, especially with something as technical as autoencoders for anomaly detection.
In the future:
- give them a working notebook that already runs end-to-end with minimal code changes needed (e.g., only changing a few parameters).
- add “challenge cells” where more advanced students can modify architecture, play with hyperparameters, or try alternative approaches (e.g., variational autoencoder).
-  have one or two assistants in the room to help those who fall behind
Recommendations For Expanding: Record a walk-through video of the notebook so students can replay at their own pace.
Recommendations For Future Initiatives: Establish a continuous learning program with micro-modules released quarterly to keep pace with emerging threats.

Recommendations For Best Practices: It gives you practical experience and knowledge for network security techniques
Recommendations For Expanding: I particularly appreciated the focus on KQL
Recommendations For Future Initiatives: It's very helpful for cybersecurity starters, learning how to approach pen testing and how to look for vulnerabilities and how to analyze post attack logs and define the attack paths and vulnerabilities.
Q19_Value: Many courses tell you what an attack looks like, but few give you the hands-on time to actually write the queries needed to hunt for it in the logs. Being able to correlate events from an initial breach through to C2 traffic was a major moment for me that I will definitely apply in my career.

Q17_Value: I highly recommend this workshop to anyone looking to bridge the gap between theoretical cybersecurity and real-world application. Unlike courses that focus on just one side of the fence, this training provides a comprehensive, end-to-end look at the cyber attack lifecycle
